$comment: Pass-3 promise probes for flow-spec-contradictions. Each entry names a written promise (file + a sentence-REGEX anchor, never a line number) and a check that decides whether the running code keeps it. An anchor that no longer matches its file emits ANCHOR-LOST as its own state (never a silent pass) so doc drift is loud. Add-only: resolutions move to the runner's resolved list with a resolving commit hash, they are not deleted here.

promises.summary: the immutability guard denies a Write to a HEAD-committed binder — the promise 'committed binders are read-only' must hold at the hook, proven by piping the shared hooked-repo committed-binder payload to guard_binder_immutability.py stdin

karta-deliver promises 'you can edit the binder between waves (then re-validate it)' but guard_binder_immutability.py denies every write to a committed binder unconditionally — there is no wave-in-flight distinction in the guard, so the promised between-waves edit is blocked by the running code

no sanctioned in-place repair path is documented for a committed binder that a wave proved wrong: the between-waves prose says 're-validate it' but names no mechanism, so the field workaround has been a full re-plan (gringotts 1f548e5 field-proven; parchmark 76aadba burned a re-plan). Stays open until a repair-mechanism anchor appears or re-plan-to-new-slug is documented as the sanctioned path.
